behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, because this is deterministic backoff math. PASS2-AUDIT: No real OS/network/subprocess needed; no model output dependency; no multi-step agent pipeline. Higher-level rejection: disqualify Integration/System because behavior text is only about retry interval calculation (doubling + cap), which can be validated at function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff policy function/object with consecutive failure counts (e.g., 1..N) and assert sequence doubles and clamps at configured max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since failure often appears at handoff between response parsing and retry policy. PASS2-AUDIT: No real network required if a local in-process transport stub provides realistic status/headers; no AI dependency; no agentic chain. Higher-level rejection: disqualify System because behavior text does not require real HTTP transport conditions, only correct interpretation of rate-limit response and wait computation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware + response classifier + backoff policy; feed simulated API responses (e.g., 429 with Retry-After, quota headers) and assert retry decision and computed delay are correct.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because resend correctness depends on interaction of scheduler, request replay, and result propagation. PASS2-AUDIT: Can be validated with in-process fake transport and controlled timers; no real OS/network requirement; no AI dependency. Higher-level rejection: disqualify System because real HTTP is not required to prove original request replay + transparent return path.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that first returns rate-limit then success; assert same request payload is resent after delay and caller receives final success value without manual retry handling.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since max-retry loop termination plus error mapping spans retry controller and error type layer. PASS2-AUDIT: No real OS/network required; deterministic stubs can drive repeated rate-limit failures; no AI dependency. Higher-level rejection: disqualify System because behavior text focuses on typed error surfaced after retry budget exhaustion, not transport-level phenomena.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to always return rate-limit failures, run client with maxRetries=N, assert exactly N retries then rejection with `QuotaExhaustedError` type and expected metadata.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System (because infinite-loop risks can involve timing/runtime behavior). PASS2-AUDIT: Demote to Integration because controlled clocks and in-process stubs can reproduce and catch non-termination in retry/quota state machine without real OS/network primitives. Higher-level rejection: disqualify System due lack of behavior text requiring real subprocess/network/filesystem effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end library modules with fake clock and always-rate-limited responses; assert bounded attempt count, terminal error emission, and no pending scheduled retries after completion.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is a shared-state coordination boundary across concurrent callers and quota manager. PASS2-AUDIT: No real external infra needed; in-process concurrency (Promises/tasks) is sufficient to expose race/state bugs; no AI dependency. Higher-level rejection: disqualify System since behavior text explicitly scopes to same process shared bucket, not OS/network-level concurrency primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Create one client instance, fire many concurrent `call()` invocations against deterministic stub responses, and assert all attempts mutate/consult one shared quota state rather than per-call isolated counters.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since FIFO correctness depends on queue implementation + dispatcher/timer interaction. PASS2-AUDIT: Can be validated entirely in-process with controlled scheduler and transport stub; no real network/OS requirement; no AI dependency. Higher-level rejection: disqualify System because behavior text is about internal dispatch ordering, not real infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue multiple calls while quota-limited, then release capacity in steps; assert transport send order matches submission order exactly.
